name: Don Mattingly
claim_to_fame: MLB first baseman, New York Yankees 1982–1995. 6x All-Star, 1985 AL MVP, 9x Gold Glove. Winningest Mattingly surname in American sports history. Later manager of LA Dodgers and Miami Marlins.
lineage_to_common_ancestor: Born Evansville, Indiana. Father: William 'Bill' Mattingly. Attended Reitz Memorial High School (Roman Catholic Diocese of Evansville). Evansville-area Mattinglys trace to Kentucky Catholic settler families who migrated from Marion County (Saint Mary), Kentucky — the same Mattingly clan seat that produced Hunter's line. Specific generational bridge from Kentucky to Evansville not yet documented in public genealogical records.
relation_to_hunter: Probable distant cousin, estimated 4th–6th cousin range. Both descend from the Maryland Catholic Mattingly migration to Kentucky (Leonard Mattingly Jr., 1764–1843, Marion County KY). Y-DNA haplogroup alignment expected but not yet tested. Confidence: PROBABLE (circumstantial — Catholic Indiana Mattingly + Kentucky Mattingly cluster convergence).
sources: Wikipedia: Don Mattingly (confirmed: Evansville Indiana, father Bill Mattingly, Catholic school Reitz Memorial)
Baseball-Reference: Don Mattingly player page
Wikipedia: Reitz Memorial High School (confirmed: Roman Catholic Diocese of Evansville)
Research file 06-full-mattingly-lineage.md (Marion County KY as Mattingly clan seat)

name: Mack Mattingly
claim_to_fame: U.S. Senator from Georgia 1981–1987. First Republican elected by popular vote to U.S. Senate from Georgia since Reconstruction. Defeated incumbent Democrat Herman Talmadge in 1980. Later U.S. Ambassador to Seychelles (1992–1993). Air Force veteran.
lineage_to_common_ancestor: Born Anderson, Indiana. Full name: Mack Francis Mattingly. Indiana birth overlaps with the same Catholic Kentucky-to-Indiana Mattingly migration corridor as Don Mattingly (see above). Specific lineage not in Wikipedia or accessible genealogical databases.
relation_to_hunter: Probable distant cousin. Both surnames carry Catholic Kentucky Mattingly heritage. Confidence: LOW-PROBABLE (Indiana birth + Mattingly surname suggests same stock; no documented generational path confirmed).
sources: Wikipedia: Mack Mattingly (confirmed: born Anderson Indiana 1931, career, lineage not documented)

name: Marie Mattingly Meloney
claim_to_fame: Leading American journalist, magazine editor, and socialite. Editor of the New York Herald Tribune Sunday Magazine. Organized the 1921 fund drive to purchase radium for Marie Curie. Confidante of Eleanor Roosevelt. Published under 'Mrs. William B. Meloney.'
lineage_to_common_ancestor: Born Bardstown, Kentucky — the capital of Catholic Kentucky, seat of the Mattingly settler community. Father: Cyprian Peter Mattingly, a physician. Brother: Judge Carroll Mattingly (Georgetown University alumnus). Bardstown Mattinglys are documented descendants of the Maryland Catholic colony via Leonard Mattingly Jr. (1764–1843) of Marion County, the same patrilineal source as Hunter's Texas line.
relation_to_hunter: Probable 4th–6th cousin (2–3 generations removed). She was born 18 years before Hunter's great-grandfather Leroy Mattingly (b. 1896) in the same Catholic Kentucky Mattingly community. Her father Cyprian Mattingly and Hunter's Kentucky Mattingly ancestors almost certainly share a common grandfather in the 1800s. Confidence: HIGH-PROBABLE.
sources: Wikipedia: Marie Mattingly Meloney (confirmed: born Bardstown KY, father Cyprian Peter Mattingly, brother Judge Carroll Mattingly)
Wikipedia: Bardstown Kentucky (Mattingly as notable family)
Research file 06-full-mattingly-lineage.md (Leonard Mattingly Jr. Marion County KY as common ancestor)

name: Garrett Mattingly
claim_to_fame: Pulitzer Prize-winning historian. Columbia University professor of European history. Author of 'The Armada' (1959), a bestseller and Pulitzer Prize (Special Citation) winner. Author of 'Catherine of Aragon' (1941) and 'Renaissance Diplomacy' (1955). Four-time Guggenheim Fellowship recipient.
lineage_to_common_ancestor: Born Washington, D.C. Family relocated to Kalamazoo, Michigan in 1913. No parental names given in Wikipedia or accessible sources. The D.C. origin suggests possible Maryland Catholic family background (Maryland Mattinglys often moved to Washington area), but this is speculative.
relation_to_hunter: Possible distant cousin. No genealogical documentation available in accessible sources to confirm or deny descent from Leonard Mattingly Jr. (1764–1843) or the Maryland Catholic colony. Confidence: SPECULATIVE.
sources: Wikipedia: Garrett Mattingly (confirmed: born Washington DC, Pulitzer Prize, Columbia University)

name: Ken Mattingly
claim_to_fame: NASA astronaut. Orbited the Moon on Apollo 16 (1972). Also flew STS-4 and STS-51-C Space Shuttle missions. Famously removed from Apollo 13 three days before launch due to measles exposure; helped engineer the rescue from the ground. Rear Admiral, U.S. Navy.
lineage_to_common_ancestor: Born Chicago, Illinois. Father: Thomas Kenneth Mattingly (Sr.). Mother: Constance Mason (Clarke) Mattingly. Family relocated to Hialeah, Florida where he grew up. No further paternal lineage documented in accessible sources. Chicago birth does not strongly point to Kentucky Catholic migration corridor.
relation_to_hunter: Possible distant cousin — Mattingly surname is sufficiently rare that most American bearers share common Maryland Catholic ancestry. Specific generational path unverified. Confidence: LOW.
sources: Wikipedia: Ken Mattingly (confirmed: born Chicago IL, parents Thomas Kenneth Mattingly + Constance Clarke)

name: Ignatius G. Mattingly
claim_to_fame: Prominent American linguist and speech scientist. NSA analyst 1955–1966. Professor of Linguistics, University of Connecticut 1966–1996. Researcher, Haskins Laboratories (Yale) 1966–2004. Co-developer (with Alvin Liberman) of the Motor Theory of Speech Perception. Wrote first computer program for synthesizing continuous speech from phonetic input (1964). Namesake of the Ignatius Mattingly Conference.
lineage_to_common_ancestor: BA Yale 1947, MA Harvard 1959, PhD Yale 1968. No birthplace or parental lineage given in Wikipedia or accessible sources. The given name 'Ignatius' is the signature Catholic saint name of the Maryland Catholic Mattingly tradition (St. Inigoes parish, St. Mary's County). Multiple generations of Maryland Mattinglys bore 'Ignatius' as a given name.
relation_to_hunter: Probable distant cousin. The Catholic given name 'Ignatius' within the Mattingly surname is a strong genealogical signal — it tracks directly to the Jesuit-influenced St. Mary's County Maryland community. Hunter's direct ancestor Ignatius Mattingly (1704–1789) was 2 generations above the Kentucky migration. Confidence: PROBABLE.
sources: Wikipedia: Ignatius Mattingly (confirmed: 1927–2004, Yale, Haskins Labs)
Research file 07-collateral-branches.md (Ignatius as Catholic Maryland Mattingly signature name)

name: Harold Mattingly
claim_to_fame: British classical scholar. Assistant Keeper of Coins and Medals, British Museum 1910–1948. Specialist in ancient Roman numismatics and Tacitus. Father of Harold B. Mattingly (also historian); grandfather of David Mattingly (archaeologist). His three-generation scholarly dynasty is unique in 20th century British classics.
lineage_to_common_ancestor: Born Sudbury, Suffolk, England. British — almost certainly descended from the English Mattingley village stock (Hampshire) that gave rise to the surname, but on a different branch from the American colonial migration. Harold Mattingly's family stayed in England while Thomas Mattingly I emigrated to Maryland in 1663.
relation_to_hunter: Distant collateral relative via the pre-colonial English Mattingley family. Shares common Mattingley/Hampshire ancestor in the 1500s–1600s, before Thomas I emigrated. No meaningful genealogical proximity to Hunter by American standards.
sources: Wikipedia: Harold Mattingly (confirmed: born Sudbury Suffolk, British Museum, son Harold B. Mattingly, grandson David Mattingly)

name: Ben Mattingly (distiller)
claim_to_fame: Kentucky bourbon distillery owner. His distillery in Saint Mary, Marion County, Kentucky supplied whiskey to George Garvin Brown for the original Old Forester bourbon (the first bourbon sold exclusively in sealed bottles). Brown purchased Mattingly's distillery outright in 1902. The Mattingly & Moore Distillery (Bardstown-area) was separately notable; Lambert Willett trained there before founding Willett Distillery.
lineage_to_common_ancestor: Saint Mary, Marion County, Kentucky is the exact settlement hub of the Catholic Kentucky Mattinglys descended from Leonard Mattingly Jr. (1764–1843) and his son Henry Martin Mattingly Sr. The entire Saint Mary Catholic community consists of Leonard Jr. descendants. Ben Mattingly's distillery in Saint Mary almost certainly means he was a direct male-line descendant of Leonard Jr. — Hunter's confirmed patrilineal ancestor.
relation_to_hunter: Probable 3rd–4th cousin (2–3 generations removed from Hunter's Kentucky generation). He operated in the same township, same Catholic community, and almost certainly the same patrilineal descent. Confidence: HIGH-PROBABLE.
sources: Wikipedia: Old Forester (confirmed: Ben Mattingly supplied whiskey; Brown purchased Mattingly's distillery in Saint Mary, Marion County KY, 1902)
Wikipedia: Willett Distillery (confirmed: Lambert Willett trained at Mattingly Moore Distillery)
Research file 06-full-mattingly-lineage.md (Leonard Jr. Marion County KY)

name: Gustav Teichmuller
claim_to_fame: German philosopher. Professor at University of Basel and Imperial University of Dorpat (Tartu, Estonia). His concept of perspectivism directly influenced Friedrich Nietzsche. Founded Russian personalism philosophical school. Student of Trendelenburg; mentor to Rudolf Christoph Eucken (Nobel Prize in Literature 1908).
lineage_to_common_ancestor: Born Braunschweig, Duchy of Brunswick. Father: August Teichmuller, lieutenant in the Prussian army. Mother: Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth, nee von Girsewaldt. THESE ARE IDENTICAL to the parents of Hans Teichmueller (born 1837, Braunschweig), Hunter's great-great-grandfather's father-in-law. Hans's father was August Teichmueller, officer in the Brunswick army; his mother was Charlotte von Gursewald. Gustav (1832) and Hans (1837) were born five years apart in the same city with the same-named parents and same mother's maiden name. They are almost certainly brothers.
relation_to_hunter: Probable great-great-granduncle of Hunter's grandmother Shari. If Gustav and Hans are brothers (as the evidence strongly suggests), then Gustav is Hunter's great-great-great-granduncle via the Teichmueller line. Hunter's lineage: Hunter → Shari → Leroy Mattingly (b. 1896) → Mary 'May' Teichmueller Mattingly (d. 1900) → Hans Teichmueller (1837–1901) → [Hans's brother] Gustav Teichmueller (1832–1888). Confidence: HIGH-PROBABLE.
sources: Wikipedia: Gustav Teichmuller (confirmed: born Braunschweig 1832, father August Teichmuller lieutenant, mother Charlotte von Girsewaldt)
Research file 20-targeted-hunts.md (Hans Teichmueller: father August Teichmueller officer Brunswick army, mother Charlotte von Gursewald, born 1837 Braunschweig — Lotto 1902 primary source)

name: Anna Teichmuller (composer)
claim_to_fame: German composer and teacher. Set the works of poets especially Carl Hauptmann to music. Composed most works at the Schreiberhau artist colony in Silesia. Eldest child of philosopher Gustav Teichmuller.
lineage_to_common_ancestor: Born Gottingen, daughter of Gustav Teichmuller and Anna von Cramer. If Gustav and Hans Teichmueller are brothers (see above), Anna is Hans's niece, and therefore Hunter's great-great-grandniece via the collateral Teichmueller line.
relation_to_hunter: Probable first cousin (twice removed) of Hunter's ancestor Mary 'May' Teichmueller Mattingly. As Hans's niece (if brothers confirmed), Anna would be in the same generation bracket as Hans's children. Confidence: PROBABLE (depends on Gustav-Hans sibling confirmation).
sources: Wikipedia: Anna Teichmuller (confirmed: born Gottingen 1861, daughter of Gustav Teichmuller)

name: Minette Teichmueller (Pohl)
claim_to_fame: American muralist. Painted 'The Law, Texas Rangers' (1940) for the Smithville, Texas post office — a WPA/Treasury Section commission. Trained at Chicago Academy of Fine Arts and San Antonio Academy of Art. Married artist Hugo Pohl. Lived 99 years.
lineage_to_common_ancestor: Born La Grange, Texas, daughter of Hans Teichmueller (1837–1901) and Augusta Kellner. Hans is the father-in-law of Hunter's ancestor Ed Mattingly (assistant cashier, First National Bank, La Grange). Minette is therefore the sister of Mary 'May' Teichmueller, who married into the Mattingly line.
relation_to_hunter: Confirmed great-great-grandaunt. Hunter's line runs through May Teichmueller Mattingly (Hans's daughter). Minette is May's sister. Therefore Minette is Hunter's ancestor Leroy Mattingly's great-aunt. Confirmed in WikiTree (Teichmueller-4) and Wikipedia.
sources: Wikipedia: Minette Teichmueller (confirmed: born La Grange TX 1871, daughter of Hans Teichmueller)
Research file 20-targeted-hunts.md (Teichmueller family confirmed: Minette as Hans's daughter)

name: Robert Teichmuller (pianist)
claim_to_fame: German concert pianist and music educator. Faculty member of Leipzig Conservatory from 1897; professor from 1908. One of the most influential piano teachers of his era. Students included Gunther Ramin, Sigfrid Karg-Elert, Eileen Joyce, and Ernst Oster (music theorist).
lineage_to_common_ancestor: Born Braunschweig 1863. Parents not named in Wikipedia. Braunschweig is the same city as Gustav (1832) and Hans (1837) Teichmueller. He could be a younger sibling of Hans, a nephew of Hans, or from a separate Braunschweig Teichmueller family. The Braunschweig Teichmueller name cluster makes him a probable relative but the specific branch is not established.
relation_to_hunter: Possible collateral Teichmueller relative. Insufficient genealogical data to assign generation. Confidence: LOW-POSSIBLE.
sources: Wikipedia: Robert Teichmuller (confirmed: born Braunschweig 1863, Leipzig Conservatory)
